plot_frame_box.description: Pixel bounding box of the visible plot region in image. Origin (0,0) is the top-left of the image; y grows downward. 'offset' is the top-left corner of the box in image coordinates; 'size' is its width and height in pixels. Detected by finding the y-axis line (left), x-axis line (bottom), and the bounding box of non-white pixels inside the L-shaped quadrant (top, right).

pixels_per_coordinate_unit.description: How many image pixels equal one unit of x or y in data coordinates.

data_to_pixel_formula.explanation: Convert any (x, y) data coordinate to a (col, row) pixel coordinate in image.png. For axes with scale='log10' the data value is first passed through log10 before the linear fit.

worked_example.scenario: Top of the Delta bar (highest in the chart).
worked_example.verification: Delta's bar top edge sits near col 423, row 73; this (col, row) should land at the top-center of the Delta bar in image.png.

detection_internals.rule: Long-run detection: longest contiguous run of pixels with gray<180 exceeding 50% of image height/width. Outer image-border lines (within 15 px of edge) excluded.
